seoTitle: How to Format Text in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Style and organize text in Microsoft Loop using the inline toolbar, keyboard shortcuts, and markdown shorthand for cleaner, readable pages.
intro: Unformatted walls of text make it hard for teammates to scan a Loop page and find what matters. This guide covers the three main ways to format text in Loop — the hover toolbar, keyboard shortcuts, and markdown-style shorthand — so your documentation stays clear and easy to read.

steps.heading: Highlight your target text
steps.body: Click into your Loop page, place your cursor on the text you want to style, and select the specific words or phrases you want to format.

steps.heading: Use the inline hover toolbar
steps.body: With text highlighted, look at the pop-up menu that appears above your selection to apply bold, italic, underline, or custom font colors.

steps.heading: Apply keyboard shortcuts
steps.body: Press Ctrl+B on Windows or Cmd+B on Mac (and similar shortcuts for italics and underline) to format text instantly without leaving the keyboard.

steps.heading: Use markdown and slash commands
steps.body: Type # or ## followed by a space to generate headings, or use a hyphen or number followed by a space to create bulleted or numbered lists.

steps.heading: Review your structured layout
steps.body: Check your finished page for consistent heading levels and list formatting, then let Loop auto-sync the styling across shared channels.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to bold text in Microsoft Loop?
faq.answer: The keyboard shortcut Ctrl+B on Windows or Cmd+B on Mac is the quickest way to bold highlighted text without opening a menu.

faq.question: Can I create headings without using the toolbar?
faq.answer: Yes, typing # followed by a space on an empty line instantly converts it into a heading using markdown shorthand.

faq.question: Does text formatting sync in real time for other collaborators?
faq.answer: Yes, Loop's multiplayer engine renders styling changes instantly across shared channels as you apply them.

faq.question: Can I copy formatting from one block to another?
faq.answer: You can use standard copy-paste to carry over formatted text, which helps match structural styling across different sections of a page.
